The sum of the five largest numbers is 0.29 of the sum of all the numbers, and the sum of the five smallest numbers is 0.26 of the sum of all the numbers. Paragraph 2: Midnight is a crucial time for analysis. It marks the point where the distribution of numbers on the board becomes more structured. \n\n  
Paragraph 3: To determine the total number of numbers on the board, we need to consider the relationships between the sums of the five largest, five smallest, and the remaining numbers. \n\n  
Paragraph 4: The remaining numbers can be calculated by subtracting the contributions of the five largest and five smallest numbers from the total sum. This gives us S - 0.29S - 0.26S = 0.45S. This means that the remaining numbers together account for 45% of the total sum.  
\n\n
